Interface RobotService

All Superinterfaces:
AutoCloseable
All Known Implementing Classes:
TcpRobotService
public interface RobotService extends AutoCloseable
An interface defining the contract for communicating with a robot. This service layer abstracts the underlying communication mechanism, allowing for sending commands and receiving responses from the robot.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    connect()
    Establishes a connection to the robot.
    void
    disconnect()
    Disconnects from the robot.
    boolean
    isConnected()
    Checks if the service is currently connected to a robot.
    String
    receive()
    Receives a response message from the robot.
    void
    send(String message)
    Sends a message (command) to the robot.

    Methods inherited from interface java.lang.AutoCloseable

    close

  • Method Details

    • connect

      void connect() throws RobotServiceException
      Establishes a connection to the robot.
      Throws:
      RobotServiceException - if the connection fails.

    • disconnect

      void disconnect()
      Disconnects from the robot.

    • send

      void send(String message) throws RobotServiceException
      Sends a message (command) to the robot.
      Parameters:
      message - The message string to send.
      Throws:
      RobotServiceException - if there is an error sending the message.

    • receive

      String receive() throws RobotServiceException
      Receives a response message from the robot.
      Returns:
      The response message as a string.
      Throws:
      RobotServiceException - if there is an error receiving the message.

    • isConnected

      boolean isConnected()
      Checks if the service is currently connected to a robot.
      Returns:
      true if connected, false otherwise.
